libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment variable is indicated. If the generated tests intentionally exercise provider discovery internals rather than real marshal/unmarshal behavior, use test-local JAXBContextFactory stubs and META-INF/services or JAXBContext.JAXB_CONTEXT_FACTORY properties, and avoid SecurityManager-based package-access tests on newer JDKs where SecurityManager behavior is terminally deprecated/fragile.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the Jakarta XML Binding reference implementation and service provider needed for ordinary JAXBContext creation, marshalling, and unmarshalling tests against the API artifact.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #4422 [Automation] java run fails for jakarta.xml.bind:jakarta.xml.bind-api:4.1.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: There is no matching org.glassfish.jaxb:jaxb-runtime:4.1.0-M1 release visible; 4.0.9 is the latest RI release and should be verified locally with Gradle/Native Image.
Adding the RI may exercise org.glassfish.jaxb runtime code and its native-image needs, so keep assertions focused on jakarta.xml.bind API behavior and rely on local verification as authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.xml.bind-api is an API artifact; normal JAXBContext/JAXB marshal/unmarshal usage requires a JAXB implementation provider because the resolved API jar contains discovery interfaces/classes but no META-INF/services provider implementation.
